response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong general software engineering skills and versatility across multiple domains, they are fundamentally misaligned for this C++ and industrial-automation-focused role. Their C++ experience is minimal and ~10+ years outdated, appearing only briefly for NI LabWindows components and a university project. The role requires deep, recent C++ expertise combined with industrial automation, IoT, and security-standards knowledge—none of which are evident in their primarily web-services and open-source platform background.
strengths: Extensive hands-on software development across multiple languages and architectures, demonstrating ability to learn and adapt to new domains
Strong culture of software quality through TDD, CI/CD, code review, and mentoring—values aligned with engineering rigor required in industrial settings
Recent experience with React, Node.js, and modern cloud tooling (AWS CDK, Kubernetes, Docker) showing comfort with contemporary technology stacks
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and outdated (10+ years ago), limited to LabWindows components and a university thesis—insufficient for primary role requirement

Career trajectory is rooted in web services, GIS platforms, and fintech rather than industrial automation, IoT, or real-time systems; no evidence of domain knowledge in manufacturing or embedded systems
No mention of security standards (SDL, IEC 62443), message brokers, multi-threaded/high-performance systems, MQTT, or industrial protocols—all core to the role

key_factors.factor: Industrial automation and IoT domain knowledge
General software engineering versatility and learning agility
